disclaimer: EXTERNAL RESEARCH - distinct from internal RFP criteria. Compiled via web research, current as of June 2026. The agent must keep these sources clearly separate from vendor self-claims and from internal Service Logic evidence, and must cite them. Analyst note: Gartner RETIRED the FSM Magic Quadrant (last 2022) and replaced it with a Market Guide (Mar 2025) - no current MQ leader/challenger rankings exist for any vendor.
rating_legend: High / Med / Low qualitative synthesis against THIS buyer's needs (60-OpCo HVAC/mechanical rollup, project billing, union labor, single-tenant, customer-built AI).
cross_cutting_flags: ServiceMax is NOT being sold by PTC - the March 2026 divestiture was ThingWorx (IoT) + Kepware, sold to TPG, not ServiceMax. ServiceMax remains PTC's SLM anchor; ownership is stable. (Corrects a common internal premise.)
Single-tenant requirement likely challenges multi-tenant SaaS vendors (Salesforce, ServiceTitan, largely BuildOps/ServiceMax) - confirm each deployment model; potentially gating.

Customer-built-AI openness: Salesforce and IFS score best; ServiceTitan's API terms explicitly RESTRICT using its data to train/benchmark AI - a material constraint given Service Logic's customer-built-AI strategy.
Native certified-payroll/CBA was not verified as native in ANY vendor; ServiceTitan via Miter partner; BuildOps markets it; IFS/Salesforce/ServiceMax need explicit RFP confirmation.

vendors.name: IFS
vendors.product: IFS Cloud / IFS Field Service Management
vendors.ownership: Private, PE-backed (EQT + Hg, TA minority; ADIA & CPP joined Apr 2025). Valued >EUR 15B. >EUR 1.2B revenue 2024, >EUR 1B ARR, >30% YoY.
vendors.analyst: Leader in all 7 Gartner FSM MQs pre-retirement; only 2024 Gartner Peer Insights Customers' Choice for FSM.
vendors.hvac_fit: High - markets FSM for construction/HVAC (distributed teams, AI scheduling, job costing).
vendors.enterprise_union: High - natively unifies ERP+EAM+FSM on one platform (unique among the five); strong multi-entity. Certified-payroll/CBA specifics unverified - probe in RFP.
vendors.project_financials: Med - full ERP with project accounting/job costing; native AIA G702/G703 and ASC 606 WIP not confirmed publicly - verify.
vendors.agentic_ai: High - acquired TheLoops to launch 'IFS Loops' agentic AI with autonomous Digital Workers; IFS Cloud 25R2 ships agentic capabilities; IFS.ai Copilot for technicians.
vendors.risks: Breadth = complexity and heavier/costlier implementation; certified-payroll/CBA and AIA specifics need confirmation.

vendors.name: Salesforce
vendors.ownership: Public mega-cap (NYSE: CRM). FY2026 revenue $41.5B; 34.1% non-GAAP op margin; record $15.0B operating cash flow; 21 straight years profitable.
vendors.analyst: Representative Vendor in the 2025 Gartner Market Guide for FSM.
vendors.hvac_fit: Med - generalist FSM, not trades-specific; lacks OOB trades/contractor templates.
vendors.enterprise_union: High - best-in-class enterprise scale/configurability, but capabilities come via configuration/customization, not prebuilt construction-labor features.
vendors.project_financials: Low - no native AIA/WIP/ASC 606; requires ERP integration or ISV add-ons.
vendors.agentic_ai: High - Agentforce/Agentforce 360; Agentforce Field Service (Esri maps, Voice-to-Form); Data Cloud. Agentforce ARR +330% YoY (>$0.5B). Open platform/APIs.
vendors.risks: Heavy integration/SI dependency (typical deployments 9-18 months, $300K-$800K implementation); no native project financials or certified payroll; multi-tenant SaaS vs. single-tenant requirement - confirm.

vendors.name: BuildOps
vendors.ownership: Private, VC-backed. $127M Series C (Mar 2025) at $1B valuation (unicorn), led by Meritech; Bond Capital and Schneider Electric's SE Ventures participated; ~$225.8M total funding. Pre-profitability/private.
vendors.analyst: Newer entrant; no Gartner MQ/Market Guide or Peer Insights Customers' Choice placement found - a gap vs. incumbents.
vendors.hvac_fit: High - purpose-built for COMMERCIAL trades (HVAC, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, fire/life-safety); 'largest commercial trade contractor platform'; veteran-owned.
vendors.enterprise_union: Med - designed for large service contracts and multi-location mgmt; integrates with Sage/Vista/QuickBooks; markets union/prevailing-wage construction payroll. Depth at 60-OpCo/3,500-tech scale UNPROVEN - verify references.
vendors.project_financials: Med-High - project mgmt + on-site invoicing; ERP integrations (Vista/Sage) typically carry AIA/WIP. Native AIA G702/G703 + ASC 606 depth not confirmed - verify.
vendors.agentic_ai: Med - markets an 'AI-driven contractor platform'; investing Series C in API architecture (positive for openness), but agentic toolset less mature than Salesforce/IFS.
vendors.risks: Youngest and smallest; least proven at this buyer's scale; no analyst validation; single-tenant and multi-entity governance need confirmation; higher vendor-stability risk despite strong backing.

vendors.name: ServiceMax
vendors.ownership: Owned by PTC (Nasdaq: PTC); acquired for $1.46B (Jan 2023). Ownership stable; PTC divested ThingWorx/Kepware (Mar 2026), NOT ServiceMax; treats ServiceMax as SLM 'anchor system of record'.
vendors.analyst: Long-standing recognized FSM vendor; listed in Gartner Peer Insights. No current MQ ranking.
vendors.hvac_fit: Med - asset-centric, OEM/equipment-oriented (asset hierarchies, SLAs, warranty, predictive maintenance), NOT contractor/trades/project-oriented.
vendors.enterprise_union: High for asset/contract mgmt at large service orgs; no evidence of native certified-payroll/CBA.
vendors.project_financials: Low - not a project-financials/AIA platform; asset-centric execution; AIA/WIP/ASC 606 needs external systems.
vendors.agentic_ai: Med - backed by PTC SLM/PLM 'digital thread'; specific ServiceMax agentic-AI products less prominent than peers - probe.
vendors.risks: Poor architectural fit (asset/OEM, not contractor/project); reported mobile (ServiceMax Go) sync reliability issues; identity tied to PTC PLM/asset strategy rather than mechanical contracting.

vendors.name: ServiceTitan
vendors.ownership: Public (Nasdaq: TTAN) since Dec 2024; IPO ~$625M, ~$8.9B valuation. FY2025 revenue $771.9M (+26%); FY2026 guide ~$895-905M; approaching non-GAAP op profitability.
vendors.analyst: Dominant in residential/commercial trades software; no current Gartner MQ ranking.
vendors.hvac_fit: High - strong trades footprint (HVAC, plumbing, electrical); historically residential-trades-centric, expanding into commercial/construction.
vendors.enterprise_union: Med - 'Enterprise Hub' centralizes multi-location/franchise mgmt; union/prevailing-wage/certified payroll via Miter partnership (NOT native); multi-legal-entity at 60-OpCo scale to be validated.
vendors.project_financials: Med-High - notably strong for a trades platform: AIA-style G702/G703 pay apps, progress billing, schedule of values, retainage, change-order tracking.
vendors.agentic_ai: Med-High capability (Titan Intelligence 'ti'; Atlas agentic sidekick) BUT API terms explicitly restrict using platform/data to train/benchmark AI - conflicts with customer-built-AI strategy.
vendors.risks: AI/data-use API restrictions; residential/light-commercial roots (large multi-month commercial project depth less proven); payroll partner-dependent (Miter); multi-tenant SaaS vs single-tenant; 60-OpCo consolidation to stress-test.
